title: acer tataricum subvar. torminaloides
intro: Acer tataricum subvar. torminaloides, also known as the Tatarian maple or torminal maple, is a subspecies of the Tatarian maple tree (Acer tataricum), native to Central Asia. This deciduous shrub or small tree is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Acer tataricum subvar. torminaloides include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
